title: Structural Steel Fusion: Single V Butt Joint
overview_text: A metallurgical engineering project demonstrating the creation of a permanent, high-strength fusion joint using Shielded Metal Arc Welding (SMAW).
problem_statement: Achieving full-penetration fusion between steel plates without causing warping or slag inclusions in structural connections.
objectives: Execute a structurally sound Single V Butt joint.
Achieve full root penetration and a continuous weld bead.
engineering_process: Beveling edges, establishing root gap, tack welding for distortion control, multi-pass welding, and mechanical slag removal.
technical_details: 5mm Mild Steel Plates; SMAW process; 3.1mm flux-coated electrodes.
design_decisions: Applied 30-degree beveling to balance penetration depth against filler consumption.
challenges: Controlling thermal distortion and ensuring complete slag removal between passes.
results: Successful full-penetration V-butt weld with uniform bead profile.
key_learning: Mastered arc stability and travel speed coordination for consistent fusion.
